seo.meta_title: Logto vs Better Auth for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: Looking for Logto vs Better Auth for your Next.js app? We compare pricing (Free – $599/mo vs Free (Open Source)), DX scores, setup time, and give you our honest pick.
seo.og_title: Logto vs Better Auth — I Tested Both So You Don't Have To
seo.og_description: Choosing auth for your Next.js app? Logto and Better Auth compared: Free – $599/mo vs Free (Open Source), 20 minutes vs 30 minutes setup.

hero.headline: Logto vs Better Auth for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Logto and Better Auth — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: I've spent time testing Logto and Better Auth with Next.js 15 App Router. Here's the short version: if you care most about Better Auth's 8/10 integration score and easy middleware setup, go with Better Auth. If Yes, up to 5,000 MAU matters more, Logto is worth a serious look.
verdict_text: Pick Better Auth if you want the smoothest Next.js auth experience. The 8/10 DX score reflects real-world App Router testing  –  middleware, server components, everything just works. Logto is a capable alternative at 7/10, particularly if you're optimizing for yes, up to 5,000 mau.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t2: Yes, fully open source with self-hosting

pros: Built-in management API and admin console
Support for social sign-in, MFA, and passwordless auth
User-friendly admin dashboard for non-technical team members
cons: Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Next.js SDK is less mature with fewer examples
Self-hosted version requires significant infrastructure setup

pros: Fully open source with no vendor lock-in or usage limits
Self-hosted on your own infrastructure - complete data control
Growing community and active development on GitHub
cons: Requires database setup and self-hosting infrastructure
Smaller ecosystem than Auth0 or Clerk
Fewer pre-built UI components compared to managed alternatives

faq.question: Should I choose Logto or Better Auth for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Logto scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. Better Auth scores 8/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Better Auth because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, up to 5,000 MAU or Yes, fully open source with self-hosting is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Logto or Better Auth more affordable?
faq.answer: Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. Better Auth starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source with self-hosting. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Logto offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Logto or Better Auth easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Logto has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Better Auth has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 30 minutes. Logto is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Logto and Better Auth together?
faq.answer: Using both Logto and Better Auth in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Logto or Better Auth?
faq.answer: Logto has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.1★. Better Auth has a Medium learning curve, 8/10, and 4.5★. On paper, Better Auth leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
